description: city in and county seat of Stanislaus County, California, United States
description_wiki: Modesto ( mə-DESS-toh; Spanish pronunciation: [moˈðesto]; Spanish for "modest") is the county seat of and the largest city in Stanislaus County, California, United States.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Colorado is a landlocked state in the Mountain West subregion of the Western United States.

description: public university in Berkeley, California, USA
description_wiki: The University of California, Berkeley (UC Berkeley, Berkeley, Cal, or California) is a public land-grant research university in the Southside and Northside neighborhoods of Berkeley, California, United States.

surface_forms: the early days of the Great Depression
description_wiki: The Great Depression was a severe global economic downturn from 1929 to 1939.

description: Public community college in Modesto, California, United States
description_wiki: Modesto Junior College (MJC) is a public community college in the College Area neighborhood of Modesto, California, United States.
General Electric Company (GE) was an American multinational conglomerate founded in 1892, incorporated in the state of New York and headquartered, during its final year of operation, in Boston.
Simon Ramo (May 7, 1913 – June 27, 2016) was an American engineer, businessman, and author.

description: university system in California, United States
description_wiki: The University of California (UC) is a public land-grant research university system in the U.S. state of California.

The National Medal of Science is an honor bestowed by the president of the United States to individuals in science and engineering who have made important contributions to the advancement of knowledge in the fields of behavioral and social sciences, biology, chemistry, engineering, mathematics and physics.

description: official residence and office of the President of the United States
description_wiki: The White House is the official residence and workplace of the President of the United States.

aliases: United States National Academy of Engineering
description: engineering branch of the United States National Academies
description_wiki: The National Academy of Engineering (NAE) is an American nonprofit, non-governmental organization.

Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers Inc
description: American professional association for electronic engineering and electrical engineering
description_wiki: The Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE) is an American 501(c)(3) charitable professional organization for electrical engineering, electronics engineering, and related disciplines.

aliases: Society for the Promotion of Engineering Education
description_wiki: The American Society for Engineering Education (ASEE) is a non-profit member association, founded in 1893, dedicated to promoting and improving engineering and engineering technology education.

description: The United States Department of War was a cabinet department that managed and oversaw the United States Army.
description_wiki: The United States Department of Defense (DOD), also referred to as the Department of War (DOW), is an executive department of the United States federal government charged with coordinating and supervising the United States Armed Forces—the Army, Navy, Marines, Air Force, Space Force, and, for some purposes, the Coast Guard—and related functions and agencies.

The National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA ) is an independent agency of the U.S. federal government responsible for the United States' civil space program and for research in aeronautics and space.
The Apollo program, also known as Project Apollo, was the United States human spaceflight program led by NASA, which landed the first humans on the Moon in 1969.

The United States National Science Foundation (NSF) is an independent agency of the United States federal government that supports fundamental research and education in all the non-medical fields of science and engineering.
